title: calophyllum teysmannii var. teysmannii
intro: Calophyllum teysmannii var. teysmannii is a plant species in the family Calophyllaceae, commonly known as the 'Yellow Balau' or 'Yellow Meranti', native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating skin conditions, wounds, and fever, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Calophyllum teysmannii var. teysmannii include wound healing, anti-aging, and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of Calophyllum teysmannii var. teysmannii typically involve extracting its essential oil or using the plant's leaves and bark in traditional medicine to create remedies for skin and wound care.
